reason: SSH StrictModes option ensures that files and ownership in the user's .ssh directory are checked before login; world-writable permissions cause login rejection. The default SSH configuration has StrictModes enabled. If not set, SSH may allow insecure configurations. To fix, add StrictModes yes to /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; filesystems affected; World-writable files in ~/.ssh; Missing StrictModes setting; Potential SSH login bypass
